items.text: 1. Solve the 6x6 Sudoku puzzle using the numbers 1 to 6.

sections.title: PART II: Counting Combinations I (5pts)
sections.instruction: Given the colors below, make the possible combinations and answer the following questions.

items.text: 2. Color the following tower of blocks with all possible combinations.
3. How many different three-block towers can you make if there is a restriction that the red block cannot be at the top?
4. How many three-block towers can you make if there is a restriction that red and blue blocks cannot be placed next to each other?
Given the colors below answer the following questions.
5. How many possible block towers can you make?
6. How many four-block towers can you make, if there is a restriction that the red block cannot be at the top?

sections.title: PART III: Counting Combinations (5pts)
sections.instruction: Given the problem below, answer the following questions.

items.text:
Suppose that the four-character code has the following restrictions:
- Numbers and letters
- Uppercase and lowercase letters
- Cannot repeat characters
7. What characters can make up the code?
8. What sets of characters can the code contain?
9. How many possible characters are there for the first spot in the password?
10. How many possible characters are there for the fourth spot in the password?
11. How many total password combinations are possible?

Suppose that the four-character code has the following restrictions:
- Numbers only
- Cannot repeat characters
12. What characters can make up the code?
13. What sets of characters can the code contain?
14. How many possible characters are there for the first spot in the password?
15. How many possible characters are there for the fourth spot in the password?
16. How many total password combinations are possible?

sections.title: PART IV: Patterns in Mathematics (5pts)
sections.instruction: Given the two numbers, find the GCF (greatest common factor) using the specified method.

items.text: 17. Use the factorization method to find the GCF of **33** and **22**.
18. Use the subtraction method to find the GCF of **25** and **50**.
19. Use the subtraction method to find the GCF of **10** and **45**.
20. Use the division method to find the GCF of **77** and **49**.
